1.25: A direct revelation: 'To the only God, our Savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord, be glory, majesty, dominion, and authority before all time and now and forever.' Jude's doxology is the most comprehensive Christological doxology in the NT — ascribing glory, majesty, dominion, and authority (four throne-room attributes of YHWH in Daniel and Isaiah) to God through Christ, and extending this across all three tenses of time (before, now, forever). The Christ through whom this glory is ascribed is the Lord whose 'love you keep yourselves in' (v. 21) and who presents believers blameless with rejoicing (v. 24). The letter's defense of the faith is framed by this doxological Christology.
